sizeRange: 200-2000 employees, Series B through late growth
geography: APAC headquartered or APAC expansion stage; serving cross-border payments and digital banks
icpNotes: Mid-market identity-verification platforms scaling APAC coverage. Microblink-shaped buyer: doc verification + biometric + risk signals, sold to neobanks, payment processors, and digital wallets. RFP cycles compressed to 60 days. The buying committee is VP Risk + Head of KYC Operations + Chief Compliance Officer.

priorities: ship a measurable reduction in onboarding friction without raising fraud loss before the next board cycle
stand up sanctions and PEP screening that holds up under MAS, BNM, and BI examination at the same time
tune the synthetic-identity model so approval rates do not drop on legitimate first-time-banked customers across APAC
give the CFO a single number for fraud-loss-as-percent-of-GMV that survives the audit committee
painPoints: vendor-stack opacity: doc verification, device fingerprinting, behavioral biometrics, AML screening, network signals, five vendors with three rule-engine syntaxes, the team cannot answer 'why did this transaction get blocked' inside any single tool
model-drift detection is manual: someone runs a query every two weeks. By the time drift surfaces, two weeks of approvals have already shipped and the chargeback-loss budget is bleeding

false-positive cost is invisible to the C-suite: a 4-point drop in approval rate looks fine on a slide, but it is the exact 4 points where Series A neobanks are bleeding to a competitor that approves first try

regulatory parallax: MAS Notice 626 wants one thing on biometric storage, GDPR another, the new PSR consent framework a third. The compliance team is reconciling the three by hand on a Confluence page

priorities: drop median onboarding time below 90 seconds for the 80th-percentile customer without dropping verification quality
consolidate the doc-verification + biometric + liveness stack into one decisioning layer the team can audit without screen-scraping vendor portals
push the analyst-review queue under 2 percent of all checks so the ops team can scale without doubling headcount
publish a clean weekly KYC ops dashboard that the COO can read in under three minutes
painPoints: manual review queue half-life: an escalation that sits in queue more than 6 hours is a customer who already abandoned. By Monday morning the team is triaging Friday-night escalations into a backlog that reps cannot recover from

vendor SLA opacity: each vendor reports decision latency in their own portal, in their own time zone, with their own definition of 'completed'. There is no single number for end-to-end onboarding latency that survives quarterly review

audit-trail hand-stitching: when a regulator asks 'why did this customer fail KYC on attempt 2 but pass on attempt 4', the ops lead pulls logs from three vendor portals plus internal tooling to reconstruct the timeline. It eats a half-day per inquiry
training-data echo: the analyst-review labels feed back into the model. Bad labels from rushed review compound across the next four weeks of approvals. Nobody has time to QA the labels
